cards.name: Vegan Bistro Donuts & Cafe
cards.address: 449 E Santa Clara St, San Jose, CA 95113, USA
cards.blurb: Kick off the morning with a donut and coffee from this highly loved vegan cafe on Santa Clara St — the donuts are the real draw, so grab a couple and take your time.
cards.why: It closes at 2 PM so hit it early before the good stuff sells out.

cards.name: State of Illusion Exhibition
cards.blurb: Swing through the State of Illusion Exhibition on your way through Santa Clara — it's a visually interesting show worth at least an hour of your afternoon.
cards.why: It's free, and daytime-only so now is the time to catch it.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Hit San Pedro Square Market for lunch and just pick whatever looks good — tacos, banh mi, ramen, it's all under one roof and you can grab a patio seat if the weather cooperates.
cards.why: Best all-in-one lunch spot downtown with no wrong answer on what to order.

cards.name: SJSU Opera Theatre presents a Double Bill: La Voix Humaine and Stuck Elevator at Hammer Theatre Center
cards.blurb: Head to Hammer Theatre for the SJSU Opera Theatre double bill — La Voix Humaine and Stuck Elevator are two very different pieces and together they make for a genuinely interesting afternoon of live performance.
cards.why: It's free and it's live opera in a great venue, which is a rare Sunday afternoon treat.

cards.name: Petroglyph Ceramic Lounge
cards.address: 1140 Lincoln Ave, San Jose, CA 95125, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down at Petroglyph Ceramic Lounge, where you pick a piece, paint it however you want, and they fire it for you — genuinely fun for adults and a great low-key way to end the afternoon.
cards.why: It's creative, relaxed, and a solid way to decompress after a full day out.

cards.name: State of Illusion Exhibition
cards.blurb: Wander through the State of Illusion Exhibition at SCU — the kind of visual art show that messes with your perception in the best way.
cards.why: It's free and a genuinely interesting way to spend a cool morning.

cards.name: Fists of Fury at King Library
cards.blurb: Head over to King Library and check out Fists of Fury, an exhibition digging into the history and culture of martial arts in America.
cards.why: Free, fascinating, and the kind of thing you'd never stumble on otherwise.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Grab lunch at San Pedro Square Market and mix and match from a dozen vendors — tacos, ramen, banh mi, whatever sounds good. Snag a patio seat and take your time.
cards.why: It's the most fun way to do lunch downtown when you can't agree on one thing.

cards.name: SJSU Student Union Bowling Center
cards.address: 211 S 9th St, San Jose, CA 95192, USA
cards.blurb: Bowl a few frames at the SJSU Student Union Bowling Center. It's a real lanes setup, not a pop-up, and it's open to the public.
cards.why: Low-key competitive and way more fun than it sounds on a Monday afternoon.

cards.name: Santana Row
cards.address: 377 Santana Row, San Jose, CA 95128, USA
cards.blurb: Stroll Santana Row in the early evening, browse the boutiques, and find a table at one of the outdoor restaurants when you're ready to sit down.
cards.why: The boulevard is relaxed on a weekday evening and the patio vibes are solid.

cards.name: LUNA Mexican Kitchen - San Jose
cards.address: 1495 The Alameda, San Jose, CA 95126, USA
cards.blurb: Wrap the night with dinner at LUNA Mexican Kitchen on The Alameda. Order margaritas and something off the dinner menu and call it a good Monday.
cards.why: Solid Mexican food with a vibe that doesn't feel rushed.

cards.name: The Midwife and The Baker
cards.address: 846 Independence Ave, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: The Midwife and the Baker is a Mountain View morning institution — grab a pastry and coffee and ease into the day.
cards.why: Hard to beat a bakery this good on a cool morning.

cards.name: Rancho San Antonio Open Space
cards.address: Rancho San Antonio County Park
cards.blurb: Hit the trails at Rancho San Antonio — the loop out to the farm overlook is a solid two hours without being brutal.
cards.why: Cool temps make this the perfect window for a hike before it gets busy.

cards.name: Volunteering at Stanford Educational Farm - Weekday
cards.address: 175 Electioneer Rd, Stanford, CA 94305
cards.blurb: Spend a couple hours volunteering at Stanford's Educational Farm, working in the fields alongside others on a weekday morning session.
cards.why: It's a genuinely satisfying way to spend time outside and give back.

cards.name: Katsu
cards.address: 859 Villa St, Mountain View, CA 94041, USA
cards.blurb: Katsu in Mountain View has an absurdly good reputation for a reason — try the local favorite and don't skip the sides.
cards.why: You'll want a real lunch after a morning of physical activity.

cards.name: 1,000 Ways to Hold: A New Work By Erika Chong Shuch
cards.address: 314 Lomita Dr, Stanford, CA 94305
cards.blurb: Erika Chong Shuch's exhibition at Stanford is an immersive new work worth exploring slowly — give yourself time to sit with it.
cards.why: Free contemporary art at Stanford is always worth the stop.

cards.name: Arisa Thai Massage
cards.address: 4546 El Camino Real Suite 203, Los Altos, CA 94022, USA
cards.blurb: Book a massage at Arisa Thai Massage in Los Altos and let someone sort out whatever the hike did to your legs.
cards.why: After a full morning outside, this is exactly what the afternoon calls for.

cards.name: Laasya Art Gallery
cards.address: 2225 E Bayshore Rd #100-7127, Palo Alto, CA 94303, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down the evening browsing Laasya Art Gallery in Palo Alto, which carries eclectic work worth more than a quick glance.
cards.why: A relaxed gallery visit is a perfect low-key cap to an active day.

cards.address: 181 Ranch Dr, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Kick off the morning at Kovai Cafe, a beloved South Indian spot in Milpitas — grab a dosa or some idli and a strong coffee to get the day going.
cards.why: It opens early and the food is genuinely excellent for a Sunday morning.

cards.name: Alum Rock Park
cards.address: 15350 Penitencia Creek Rd, San Jose, CA 95127, USA
cards.blurb: Head out to Alum Rock Park and walk the creek trails through the foothills. It's a real hike with some payoff views, not just a flat loop.
cards.why: Low 50s at 9 AM warming up to 75 makes this the sweet spot for a morning hike.

cards.name: The Orange Tabby
cards.address: 1344 Ridder Park Dr, San Jose, CA 95131, USA
cards.blurb: The Orange Tabby is a cafe with a cult following. Grab lunch and linger a bit — the vibe is cozy and the food hits.
cards.why: It closes at 4 PM so Sunday lunch is the move.

cards.name: EDscapade Games
cards.address: 1150 Murphy Ave #200, San Jose, CA 95131, USA
cards.blurb: EDscapade Games has some of the most creative escape rooms in the area. Book a room and see if you and your crew can actually solve it.

cards.address: 809 Cuesta Dr, Mountain View, CA 94040, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning here with pastries and coffee. GK Pastry is the kind of place you linger in — grab something sweet and settle in before the day picks up.
cards.why: It's a perfect low-key Sunday morning opener and they're open early.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Spend a couple hours at the Computer History Museum — even if you're not a tech person, the exhibits are genuinely fascinating. The Steve Jobs in Exile display is worth the extra look.
cards.why: Two hours goes fast here and you'll leave with stuff to talk about.

cards.name: Mountain View Farmers Market

cards.blurb: Wander the Mountain View Farmers Market and pick up whatever looks good — local produce, flowers, snacks, the works. No agenda needed, just browse.
cards.why: It's a great way to ease into a Sunday without rushing anywhere.

cards.name: Shoreline Park & Lake
cards.address: Shoreline at Mountain View
cards.blurb: Take a post-museum walk around Shoreline Park and Lake. The 74°F afternoon is going to be ideal out here — big open views, bay breezes, easy flat paths.
cards.why: You're already right there and the afternoon weather is going to be gorgeous.

cards.name: Boba Bliss
cards.address: 685 San Antonio Rd Suite 15, Mountain View, CA 94040, USA
cards.blurb: Swing by Boba Bliss for a drink and a snack break — good spot to recharge before the afternoon stretch.
cards.why: It's the right call between a walk and your next stop.

cards.name: Massage Envy
cards.address: 1040 Grant Rd #110, Mountain View, CA 94040, USA
cards.blurb: Book a massage at Massage Envy and actually decompress for a bit. This is adults-only time well spent on a slow Sunday.
cards.why: A Sunday without a little self-care is just a regular day.

cards.name: Leleka Ukrainian Comfort Food (Sunnyvale)
cards.address: 1026 W Evelyn Ave Kitchen#114, Sunnyvale, CA 94086, USA
cards.blurb: End the day with dinner at Leleka Ukrainian Comfort Food. Hearty, soulful dishes that hit differently after a full day out.
cards.why: It's some of the most interesting comfort food you'll find in the South Bay.

cards.name: California Ave Farmers Market
cards.address: California Ave, Palo Alto
cards.blurb: Wander the stalls on California Ave, grab a coffee from one of the vendors, and pick up something fresh for snacking — it's a great low-key way to kick off a Sunday morning.
cards.why: Sunday farmers markets are hard to beat when the weather's good and you've got nowhere to rush.

cards.name: The Dish Trail
cards.address: Stanford Foothills (Stanford Ave trailhead)
cards.blurb: Lace up and tackle The Dish loop up in the Stanford foothills. The views open up fast once you're on the ridge, and the trail is busy enough to feel social but big enough to find your own pace.
cards.why: It's the iconic South Bay hike and a Sunday morning on the Dish just feels right.

cards.name: Bevri - Palo Alto
cards.address: 335 University Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Post-hike lunch at Bevri on University Ave. Georgian food is the move here — think khachapuri, dumplings, and a glass of natural wine if you're feeling it.
cards.why: Georgian cuisine is genuinely fun and Bevri does it well without being fussy about it.

cards.name: Gallery Conversations | Jeremy Frey: Woven
cards.address: 328 Lomita Drive, Stanford, CA 94305
cards.blurb: Head over to the Cantor Arts Center for the Gallery Conversations around Jeremy Frey's woven works. Frey's basketry pushes way past craft into something that makes you stop and look twice.
cards.why: Free, beautiful, and way more interesting than it sounds on paper.

cards.name: Highlights Tour | Anderson Collection
cards.address: 314 Lomita Dr, Stanford, CA 94305
cards.blurb: While you're already at Stanford, swing through the Anderson Collection's Highlights Tour. The postwar American art here is genuinely world-class and the building itself is stunning.
cards.why: You're already on campus and this is one of the best free art collections in California.

cards.name: Tong Sui Desserts & Drinks (Palo Alto)
cards.address: 540 Bryant St, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down with something sweet at Tong Sui in downtown Palo Alto. Order a grass jelly dessert drink or a warm tong sui bowl and decompress after a full day on your feet.
cards.why: It's a perfect low-key stop between afternoon and dinner when you want something light and different.

cards.name: Hiroshi
cards.address: 328 Main St, Los Altos, CA 94022, USA
cards.blurb: Dinner at Hiroshi in Los Altos. This is a serious omakase experience, so settle in, let the chef lead, and don't rush it. The fish here is exceptional.
cards.why: If you're going to splurge on a Sunday dinner, this is exactly the kind of meal worth it.

cards.name: 1 Oz Coffee
cards.address: 3051 Tasman Dr, Santa Clara, CA 95054, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning with a solid cup at 1 Oz Coffee — good espresso, chill vibe, exactly the right way to ease into a Sunday.
cards.why: Every good day starts with decent coffee.

cards.name: State of Illusion Exhibition
cards.blurb: Swing through the State of Illusion Exhibition at SCU — the kind of show that makes you stop and actually look at things twice.
cards.why: Free art on a crisp Sunday morning is a pretty good deal.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Hit the Triton Museum of Art for the contemporary galleries and then wander the sculpture garden out back while the temperature climbs toward that 74-degree high.
cards.why: It's genuinely one of the most underrated spots in the South Bay and nobody's ever crowded out of it.

cards.name: Santana Row
cards.address: 377 Santana Row, San Jose, CA 95128, USA
cards.blurb: Walk Santana Row when the afternoon sun hits it right, browse the boutiques, and scope out a patio table at Straits or Zinnia for an early dinner.
cards.why: The boulevard is at its best on a warm Sunday afternoon.

cards.name: Rosicrucian Egyptian Museum
cards.address: 1660 Park Ave, San Jose, CA 95191, USA
cards.blurb: Head to the Rosicrucian Egyptian Museum for real mummies, actual hieroglyphics, and a surprisingly deep collection that feels totally out of place in a good way.
cards.why: It's one of those places you keep meaning to go and then suddenly an afternoon frees up.

cards.name: Izzy's Brooklyn Bagels
cards.address: 477 California Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94306, USA
cards.blurb: Start the day with a fresh bagel and coffee at Izzy's — it's a California Ave staple and the perfect low-key morning fuel before a full day out.
cards.why: Hard to beat a good bagel on a crisp Sunday morning.

cards.name: Gallery Conversations | Jeremy Frey: Woven
cards.address: 328 Lomita Drive, Stanford, CA 94305
cards.blurb: Head over to the Cantor Arts Center for Gallery Conversations on Jeremy Frey's Woven — his basketry work is genuinely stunning and the docent conversations add a lot of context.
cards.why: Free art on a Sunday with a Stanford backdrop is hard to argue with.

cards.name: California Ave Farmers Market
cards.address: California Ave, Palo Alto
cards.blurb: Stroll the California Ave Farmers Market while the morning is still cool — pick up some local produce, sample whatever's out, and take your time wandering the stalls.
cards.why: You're already on California Ave, so this is the perfect next move.

cards.name: Highlights Tour | Anderson Collection
cards.address: 314 Lomita Dr, Stanford, CA 94305
cards.blurb: Pop next door to the Anderson Collection for the Highlights Tour while you're already in the mood — it's one of the best collections of American modern art you'll find anywhere.
cards.why: Both Stanford galleries are steps apart and both are free, so doing them back-to-back just makes sense.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Spend a couple hours at the Computer History Museum — grab lunch from whatever's available there, then dig into the exhibits on the entire arc of computing history from punch cards to modern AI.
cards.why: Even if you're not a tech person, the stories in here are genuinely wild.

cards.name: Hiroshi
cards.address: 328 Main St, Los Altos, CA 94022, USA
cards.blurb: End the night at Hiroshi on Main Street in Los Altos — this is a proper splurge dinner, so settle in, order the omakase or chef's menu, and let the evening stretch out.
cards.why: You earned a great meal after a full day, and Hiroshi is as good as it gets locally.

cards.name: 1 Oz Coffee
cards.address: 3051 Tasman Dr, Santa Clara, CA 95054, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning with a solid cup at 1 Oz Coffee — good vibes, low-key atmosphere, and the kind of place you actually want to sit in for a bit.
cards.why: Perfect way to ease into a Sunday before the day picks up.

cards.name: Spring Valley Trail
cards.address: Spring Valley Trail, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Hit Spring Valley Trail while the morning is still cool and crisp — it's a great stretch of trail with open views and a good pace for a casual hike.
cards.why: The weather is ideal for it, and you'll have worked up an appetite by the time you're done.

cards.name: Miles Bistro
cards.address: 1525 Landess Ave #141, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Miles Bistro is the kind of spot that surprises you — grab a table, take your time, and try whatever the kitchen is putting out that day.
cards.why: It's a Milpitas gem that doesn't get nearly enough attention.

cards.name: Claw Meee
cards.address: 532 Barber Ln, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Claw Meee is exactly the kind of low-stakes, high-fun afternoon stop you didn't know you needed — give the claw machines a serious go and see who does better.
cards.why: It's genuinely fun and a little ridiculous, which is the point.

cards.name: Jandee Thai Massage
cards.address: 1699 N Capitol Ave #50, San Jose, CA 95132, USA
cards.blurb: Book a session at Jandee Thai Massage and actually decompress for an hour and a half — proper Thai massage, good pressure, no spa fluff.
cards.why: You've been on your feet all morning, this is the move.

cards.name: Saucy Asian - Milpitas
cards.address: Food Court, 447 Great Mall Dr Space 10, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Swing through Saucy Asian in the Great Mall food court for a late afternoon bite — it's casual, the food hits, and it's a solid way to tide you over before dinner.
cards.why: Sometimes the food court spot is genuinely the best call, and this one delivers.

cards.name: Ichika
cards.address: 1728 N Milpitas Blvd, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: End the night at Ichika for a proper sit-down dinner — it opens at 5 so snag an early table, order whatever looks good, and take your time winding down.
cards.why: It's the kind of dinner spot that makes Sunday feel like it actually meant something.
